content.text:
Module:
Virtualization, Cloud Computing and Security  |
Semester:
3  |
Type:
UEF31
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
4h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
3h
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
3  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Familiarize students with Cloud Computing, presenting the foundations of virtualization and tools for creating and deploying Cloud infrastructures
Introduce security applications in a cloud environment
Recommended Prerequisites
Notions of virtualization, distributed computing, networking, web technologies
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Virtualization
Definitions of virtualization
Virtual machines (workstation virtualization, server virtualization)
Application virtualization
Data and storage virtualization
Network virtualization
Definitions and History
Definitions: The Cloud and Cloud Computing from economic and virtual space perspectives
History: 1950s, early 2000s...
Cloud Computing Models and Services
Cloud models
Cloud services: IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Cloud services architecture
Other services
Cloud Computing Architecture and Topology
Architecture: N-Tiers, SOA, virtual machines, file virtualization
Deployment: pilot, deployment and integration, load management phases
Topology: private, public, community, hybrid, distributed, Inter-Cloud, Multi-Cloud
Cloud Examples
DROPBOX, Microsoft Cloud Platform, commercial clouds and main market players
OpenStack, OpenNebula presentation
Storage cloud examples
Cloud Advantages and Limitations
Advantages: cost reduction, flexibility and business focus
Limitations: data loss control, data security issues
Cloud Security and Confidentiality
General aspects, specific security problems, contractual aspects
Security best practices, threat types, attacker types, security risks, risk mitigation tips
Assessment
Exam: 60%
Continuous assessment: 40%
Bibliographic References
Rajkumar Buyya, James Broberg, Andrzej M. Goscinski, "Cloud Computing: Principles and Paradigms", John Wiley & Sons, 2010.
Lee Gillam, "Cloud computing", Springer, 2010.
Zaigham Mahmood, Richard Hill, "Cloud Computing for Enterprise Architectures", Springer, 2011.
Cigref, "Fondamentaux du Cloud Computing", Mars 2013.
Romain Hennion, Hubert Tournier, Eric Bourgeois, Cloud Computing: Décider - Concevoir - Piloter - Améliorer, Eyrolles, 2012.
Guillaume Plouin, Cloud Computing, Sécurité, stratégie d'entreprise et panorama du marché, Dunod, 2013.
Guillaume Plouin, Tout sur le Cloud Personnel, Dunod, 2013.
Adora Nwodo, Confident Cloud, Kogan Page, 2024.
Naresh Kumar Sehgal et al., Cloud Computing with Security and Scalability, Edition 3, Springer, 2023.
Matthew Portnoy, Virtualization Essentials, Sybex, 2023.
Souvik Pal et al., Cloud Computing Solutions: Architecture, Data Storage, Implementation, and Security, Wiley, 2022.
Judith S. Hurwitz, Daniel Kirsch, Cloud Computing For Dummies, 2nd Edition, Wiley, 2020.
